rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Abatement Of Fraud Action
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Even assuming it is proper to ask this department to abate the susequently filed complaint in another department, the petition in this department is no longer pending, does not involve the same parties, and could not award the relief sought in the civil action. Exclusive concurrent jurisdiction acts to stay actions in a different superior court as opposed to different departments of the same superior court. In any event, for the same reasons that abatement would not be appropriate, staying on the basis of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is also not appropriate in this case. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise opposing parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Wednesday, December 10, 2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMUNDO LUNA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Petitioner Ignacio Luna, Jr., For Reconsideration Of Order Re Ignacio Luna Jr.S First And Final Account; For Allowance Of .Trustee Fees; For Attorney Fee And Reimbursement Of Costs Or, In The Alternative, For A Modification Of Said Order To Conform To Final Accounting; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Ignacio Luna, Jr.; Declarat
rulings.Rulings: The Court grants the request to credit Ignacio Luna with the ILIT premium refunds of $9,020 based upon Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094 and Marriage of Barthold (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1301. The Court denies the request contained in the Amended Motion for Reconsideration for an additional credit of $6,204.99 as untimely and for failure to meet the threshhold showing necessary for reconsideration. The Court will correct the mathemathical errors in its 8/13/2014 order on page 2 and page 4, line 9 (actual figure should be $77,228) resulting in a balance of $39,169.01 and send an Amended Order. Any party requesting argument must notify the court (415-551-3702) and all other parties by 4:00 p.m. on 12/10/2014.
